title: Utilities Enterprise Asset Management Platform Evaluation: 2026 Electric, Water, and Gas EAM System Selection
description: Comprehensive evaluation of 2026 utilities enterprise asset management platforms, covering enterprise asset management (EAM), equipment maintenance managem.
content:
Utilities Enterprise Asset Management Platform Evaluation: 2026 Electric, Water, and Gas EAM System Selection

Scale and Complexity of Utility Enterprise Asset Management

Electric, water, and gas companies have massive asset scales (hundreds of thousands of assets), complex asset types (equipment, facilities, vehicles, tools), widely distributed assets (substations, pipelines, water plants, stations), and important asset maintenance (preventive maintenance, fault repairs, condition monitoring). Traditional asset management relies on paper records and dispersed systems, resulting in incomplete asset registries, delayed maintenance plans, and difficult asset performance assessment. Utilities enterprise asset management platforms (EAM) achieve digitized asset registries, integrated maintenance management, asset performance analysis, and investment decision support, improving asset utilization, optimizing maintenance efficiency, and supporting asset decisions.

Utilities enterprise asset management core features include asset registry management, maintenance management, asset performance analysis, investment decision support, and data analysis. Utilities EAM platform selection requires evaluating asset registry completeness, maintenance management depth, performance analysis capabilities, investment decision support, and data analysis depth.

In-depth Evaluation: Mainstream Utilities Enterprise Asset Management Platforms

1. IBM Maximo for Utilities

IBM Maximo is the benchmark for utilities enterprise asset management, leading in asset management and maintenance management.

Core Features:

  • Asset Registry: Digitized asset registry management
  • Maintenance Management: Preventive maintenance and fault repairs
  • Asset Performance: Asset performance analysis
  • Investment Decision: Asset investment decision support
  • Data Analysis: Asset data analytics
  • Mobile Applications: Mobile asset management applications
  • Condition Monitoring: Asset condition monitoring integration

Technical Features: Leading asset management and maintenance management, suitable for large utility companies.

Price Range: EAM platform $100,000-$500,000.

2. Infor EAM for Utilities

Infor EAM utilities asset management platform integrates asset registry with maintenance management, providing comprehensive EAM.

Core Features:

  • Asset Registry: Asset registry management
  • Maintenance Management: Maintenance management
  • Asset Performance: Asset performance analysis
  • Data Analysis: Asset data analytics

Price Range: EAM platform $50,000-$200,000.

3. SAP Enterprise Asset Management

SAP enterprise asset management integrates ERP integration, achieving asset and financial integration.

Core Features:

  • Asset Registry: Asset registry management
  • Maintenance Management: Maintenance management
  • ERP Integration: SAP ERP integration
  • Data Analysis: Asset data analytics

Price Range: EAM module $50,000-$200,000.

4. Oracle Enterprise Asset Management

Oracle enterprise asset management integrates asset registry with maintenance, providing comprehensive asset management.

Core Features:

  • Asset Registry: Asset registry management
  • Maintenance Management: Maintenance management
  • Data Analysis: Asset data analytics
  • Oracle Integration: Oracle system integration

Price Range: EAM module $50,000-$200,000.

5. Asset Performance Management Platform

Asset performance management platform focuses on asset performance analysis, specialized in performance analysis.

Core Features:

  • Asset Performance: Asset performance analysis
  • Performance Metrics: Asset performance metrics
  • Data Analysis: Performance data analytics

Price Range: Performance management platform $30,000-$150,000.

6. Utility Maintenance Management System

Utility maintenance management system focuses on maintenance management, specialized in maintenance management.

Core Features:

  • Maintenance Management: Preventive maintenance and fault repairs
  • Maintenance Plans: Maintenance plan management
  • Data Analysis: Maintenance data analytics

Price Range: Maintenance management system $30,000-$150,000.

7. Asset Investment Decision Tool

Asset investment decision tool focuses on asset investment decision support, specialized in investment decisions.

Core Features:

  • Investment Decision: Asset investment decision support
  • Investment Analysis: Investment return analysis
  • Data Analysis: Investment data analytics

Price Range: Investment decision tool $20,000-$100,000.

8. Utility Mobile Asset App

Utility mobile asset app focuses on mobile asset management, providing mobile asset management.

Core Features:

  • Mobile Assets: Mobile asset management
  • Mobile Maintenance: Mobile maintenance records
  • Data Analysis: Mobile data analytics

Price Range: Mobile asset app $10,000-$50,000.

9. Asset Condition Monitoring Platform

Asset condition monitoring platform focuses on asset condition monitoring, specialized in condition monitoring.

Core Features:

  • Condition Monitoring: Asset condition monitoring
  • Condition Analysis: Condition data analytics
  • Data Analysis: Condition data analytics

Price Range: Condition monitoring platform annual fee $10,000-$50,000.

10. Utility EAM Analytics Tool

Utility EAM analytics tool focuses on asset data analysis, specialized in EAM analysis.

Core Features:

  • EAM Analysis: Asset data analytics
  • Visualization: Asset data visualization
  • Performance Analysis: Asset performance analysis

Price Range: EAM analytics tool annual fee $10,000-$50,000.

EAM AnalyticsBasicBasicPerformance AnalysisInvestment AnalysisAnalysis ProfessionalBasic10-50 annual

Selection Strategy and Scenario Matching

Scenario 1: Large Utility EAM

Asset registry, maintenance management, and performance analysis all covered. IBM Maximo enterprise asset management leads.

Scenario 2: Comprehensive EAM

Asset registry and maintenance management comprehensive. Infor EAM provides comprehensive EAM.

Scenario 3: ERP Integrated EAM

EAM and ERP system integration. SAP EAM ERP integration comprehensive.

Scenario 4: Asset Performance Analysis

Asset performance analysis and metrics management. Asset Performance specialized in performance analysis.

Scenario 5: Maintenance Management Professional

Preventive maintenance and fault repair management. Maintenance System specialized in maintenance management.

Scenario 6: Asset Investment Decision

Asset investment decision support. Investment Decision specialized in investment decisions.

Scenario 7: Mobile Asset Management

Mobile asset management and maintenance. Mobile Asset provides mobile asset management.

Scenario 8: Asset Condition Monitoring

Asset condition monitoring and analysis. Condition Monitoring specialized in condition monitoring.

2026 Enterprise Asset Management Development Trends

Trend 1: Mobilized Asset Management

Asset management shifting from desktop to mobile, mobile asset efficiency improved. Mobile Asset provides mobilization.

Trend 2: Preventive Maintenance

Maintenance management shifting from fault-based to preventive, maintenance efficiency improved. Maintenance System provides preventive maintenance.

Trend 3: Visualized Asset Performance

Asset performance shifting from numerical to visual display, performance intuitive display. Asset Performance provides performance visualization.

Trend 4: Data-driven Investment Decisions

Asset investment decisions shifting from experience to data analysis, decision accuracy improved. Investment Decision provides data-driven decisions.

Trend 5: Intelligent Condition Monitoring

Asset condition monitoring shifting from simple to intelligent, monitoring precision improved. Condition Monitoring provides intelligent monitoring.

Implementation Recommendations and Risk Considerations

Complete Asset Registry: Asset registry needs to cover all assets.

Reasonable Maintenance Plans: Maintenance plans need to verify reasonableness.

Scientific Performance Metrics: Asset performance metrics need scientific design.

Accurate Investment Decisions: Investment decisions need to verify accuracy.

Adapted Mobile Applications: Mobile asset apps need to adapt to field use.

Summary

The 2026 utilities enterprise asset management platform market has formed eight categories: enterprise asset management leading, comprehensive EAM, ERP integrated EAM, professional performance analysis, professional maintenance management, professional investment decision, mobile asset management, and professional condition monitoring. Enterprise asset management selects IBM Maximo; comprehensive EAM uses Infor EAM; ERP integration chooses SAP EAM; performance analysis uses Asset Performance; maintenance management chooses Maintenance System; investment decision uses Investment Decision; mobile asset management selects Mobile Asset; condition monitoring uses Condition Monitoring. Selection should focus on maintenance management as core, match asset scale and performance needs, and gradually introduce preventive maintenance and mobile asset management.
author: UtilitiesEAM Team
